user_query:
I need a lightweight document pack built from two HTML sources, but please run it through a PDF-centric pipeline behind the scenes for consistency. Read these inputs:
- input/company_policy.html
- input/meeting_notes.html

Tasks and deliverables (use only relative paths):
1) For each HTML file, convert it to PDF and then extract text from the resulting PDF. Save the extracted texts to:
   - output/text/policy_text.txt
   - output/text/notes_text.txt
   Important: Do not include any .pdf files in output/; PDFs are intermediate only.

2) Create a merged text file at output/text/merged_text.txt that concatenates the two extracted texts in this order, with clear section headers exactly as:
   === COMPANY POLICY ===
   [policy text]
   === MEETING NOTES ===
   [notes text]

3) Build a single combined HTML document at output/html/combined.html with an H1 "Combined Document" and two sections titled "Company Policy" and "Meeting Notes" containing the corresponding content.

4) Produce an executive summary for an executive audience (150–250 words) that highlights the most important policy changes and the key meeting decisions. Save it to output/report/executive_summary.md.

5) Create a manifest at output/report/manifest.json with this structure:
   {
     "sources": {
       "policy_html": "input/company_policy.html",
       "notes_html": "input/meeting_notes.html"
     },
     "operations": {
       "policy": ["html_to_pdf", "pdf_to_text"],
       "notes": ["html_to_pdf", "pdf_to_text"]
     },
     "used_pdf_pipeline": true,
     "merge_strategy": "concatenate_with_headers",
     "summary": {
       "word_count": <integer word count of executive_summary.md>
     }
   }

Notes:
- Do not place any .pdf files in output/.
- Ensure the extracted text still includes recognizable headings (e.g., policy sections and an "Action items" list from the meeting notes) so we can verify the content survived the PDF round-trip.
- Use only .txt, .html, .md, and .json files for deliverables under output/.
